title: The Future of AI — Memory, AGI, and Beyond

scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: The future of AI. [pause] What is real today. What is coming. And what remains speculation.

scenes.name: Three buckets
scenes.text: Three categories to keep separate. [pause] Real and available — narrow AI. Being developed — agents and memory. Theoretical — AGI and superintelligence.

scenes.name: Memory
scenes.text: <emphasis>Memory</emphasis> is the newest dimension. [pause] Context lasts one conversation. Memory lasts across them. [pause] AI starts to remember your subject, your grade, your style.

scenes.name: Memory caveats
scenes.text: Memory is not human memory. [pause] It is selective — it stores what it judges important. It is limited. It is manageable — you can edit it. [pause] And it can be wrong.

scenes.name: AGI
scenes.text: <emphasis>AGI</emphasis> — artificial general intelligence. [pause] AI as flexible as a human across any domain. [pause] It does not exist yet. There is no agreed timeline. Today's tools are still narrow AI.

scenes.name: ASI
scenes.text: <emphasis>ASI</emphasis> goes further — superintelligence beyond any human. [pause] This is theoretical. Popular culture and policy debate. [pause] No bearing on your classroom in the foreseeable future.

scenes.name: Coming next
scenes.text: What's actually coming in two to five years. [pause] Better memory. More capable agents. Deeper integration in tools you already use. [pause] And more platforms built specifically for education.

scenes.name: Prepare
scenes.text: How to prepare. [pause] Build a strong foundation now. Develop critical evaluation. Stay curious — but skeptical. [pause] Focus on what AI cannot replace: connection, judgment, the human qualities that matter most.
